info.name: VectorPath
info.comment: Extends the Vector class with support for generating custom paths.

description.p: VectorPath provides support for parsing SVG styled path strings.

action.name: Flush
action.comment: Flush buffered data from an object.

action.name: Move
action.comment: Moves the path to a new position.
action.prototype: ERR acMove(*Object, DOUBLE DeltaX, DOUBLE DeltaY, DOUBLE DeltaZ)

input.param: The number of units to move along the X axis.
The number of units to move along the Y axis.
The number of units to move along the Z axis.

action.name: MoveToPoint
action.comment: Moves the path to a new fixed position.
action.prototype: ERR acMoveToPoint(*Object, DOUBLE X, DOUBLE Y, DOUBLE Z, MTF Flags)

input.param: The new X position to move the object to.
The new Y position to move the object to.
The new Z position to move the object to.
Set the relevant MTF flag for each provided parameter.

description.p: This action updates the X and Y placement fields. The path commands remain unchanged.

method.name: AddCommand
method.comment: Add one or more commands to the end of the path sequence.
method.prototype: ERR vp::AddCommand(OBJECTPTR Object, std::span<const struct PathCommand> Commands)

input.param: Array of commands to add to the path.

description.p: This method will add a series of commands to the end of a Vector's existing path sequence. The commands must be provided as a sequential array. No checks will be performed to confirm the validity of the sequence.
Calling this method will also result in the path being recomputed for the next redraw.

method.name: GetCommand
method.comment: Retrieve a specific command from the path sequence.
method.prototype: ERR vp::GetCommand(OBJECTPTR Object, INT Index, struct PathCommand ** Command)

input.param: The index of the command to retrieve.
The requested command will be returned in this parameter.

description.p: Calling GetCommand() will return a direct pointer to the command identified at Index. The pointer will remain valid for as long as the VectorPath is not modified.

method.name: RemoveCommand
method.comment: Remove at least one command from the path sequence.
method.prototype: ERR vp::RemoveCommand(OBJECTPTR Object, INT Index, INT Total)

input.param: The index of the command to remove.
The total number of commands to remove, starting from the given Index.

description.p: This method will remove a series of commands from the current path, starting at the given Index. The total number of commands to remove is indicated by the Total parameter.

method.name: SetCommand
method.comment: Copies one or more commands into an existing path.
method.prototype: ERR vp::SetCommand(OBJECTPTR Object, INT Index, std::span<const struct PathCommand> Command)

input.param: The index of the command that is to be set.
An array of commands to set in the path.

description.p: Use SetCommand() to copy one or more commands into an existing path.

method.name: SetCommandList
method.comment: The fastest available mechanism for setting a series of path instructions.
method.prototype: ERR vp::SetCommandList(OBJECTPTR Object, std::span<const struct PathCommand> Commands)

description.p: Use SetCommandList() to copy a series of path commands to a VectorPath object. All existing commands will be cleared as a result of this process.

field.name: Commands
field.comment: Direct pointer to the PathCommand array.

description.p: Read the Commands field to obtain a direct pointer to the PathCommand array. This will allow the control points of the path to be modified directly, but it is not possible to resize the path. After making changes to the path, call Flush to register the changes for the next redraw.
This field can also be written at any time with a new array of PathCommand structures. Doing so will clear the existing path, if any.

field.name: PathLength
field.comment: Calibrates the user agent's distance-along-a-path calculations with that of the author.

description.p: The author's computation of the total length of the path, in user units. This value is used to calibrate the user agent's own distance-along-a-path calculations with that of the author. The user agent will scale all distance-along-a-path computations by the ratio of PathLength to the user agent's own computed value for total path length. This feature potentially affects calculations for text on a path, motion animation and various stroke operations.

field.name: Sequence
field.comment: A sequence of points and instructions that will define the path.

description.p: The Sequence is a string of points and instructions that define the path. It is based on the SVG standard for the path element d attribute, but also provides some additional features that are present in the vector engine. Commands are case insensitive.
description.pre: M: Move To L: Line To V: Vertical Line To H: Horizontal Line To Q: Quadratic Curve To T: Quadratic Smooth Curve To C: Curve To S: Smooth Curve To A: Arc Z: Close Path
description.p: The use of lower case characters will indicate that the provided coordinates are relative (based on the coordinate of the previous command).
To terminate a path without joining it to the first coordinate, omit the Z from the end of the sequence.

field.name: TotalCommands
field.comment: The total number of points defined in the path sequence.

description.p: The total number of points defined in the path Sequence is reflected in this field. Modifying the total directly is permitted, although this should be used for shrinking the list because expansion will create uninitialised command entries.

field.name: X
field.comment: The left-side of the path. Can be expressed as a fixed or scaled coordinate.

description.p: Setting X moves the computed path so that its left-most boundary aligns with the supplied coordinate. The path commands remain unchanged.

field.name: Y
field.comment: The top of the path. Can be expressed as a fixed or scaled coordinate.

description.p: Setting Y moves the computed path so that its top-most boundary aligns with the supplied coordinate. The path commands remain unchanged.

struct.name: PathCommand
struct.field: Equivalent to the large-arc-flag in SVG, it ensures that the arc follows the longest drawing path when TRUE.
Equivalent to the sweep-flag in SVG, it inverts the default behaviour in generating arc paths.
The targeted X coordinate (absolute or scaled) for the command
The targeted Y coordinate (absolute or scaled) for the command
The X2 coordinate for curve commands, or RX for arcs
The Y2 coordinate for curve commands, or RY for arcs
The X3 coordinate for curve-to or smooth-curve-to
The Y3 coordinate for curve-to or smooth-curve-to
